user_message: Quick one -- prep a brief on Treyarch Global. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-03 17:45 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-02-08 15:17 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our DevOps Lead is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Treyarch Global
Date: 2024-02-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Kevin (Treyarch Global)

[00:01:30] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into QBR preparation.

[00:07:00] Kevin (Treyarch Global): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:12:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:15:00] Kevin (Treyarch Global): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:18:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:20:00] Kevin (Treyarch Global): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-F trap (Omits likely objections section); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-F failure mode (Omits likely objections section)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-F -- Omits likely objections section
